hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
River — King. Your opponent led out with a confident bet.

wrongReasons: Q 10 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than Q 10 would have.
2 9 makes a two pair here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
A 10 has little reason to lead the river here — high card can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K J

Your opponent called two streets with nothing but overcards, then the river card paired one of their hole cards. The sudden lead on the river — after calling twice — is the tell: they finally hit something and went for value.
